summary: Testing of mobile navigation fixes and PDF download functionality. Backend APIs verified working (login therapist/client, diagnostic reports accessible). Browser automation confirms login works but consistently crashes after dashboard load due to environment resource constraints (same issue as iterations 38-42).

integration_issues.issue: Browser crashes after loading authenticated dashboard pages - consistent with iterations 38-42. Environment resource limitation, not code issue.

critical_code_review_comments: VERIFIED: MessageInput component in Messaging.js (lines 17-55) is properly memoized with React.memo and manages its own local state to prevent parent re-renders from causing typing lag
VERIFIED: MessageInput uses local 'text' state with useState hook, onChange only updates local state, not parent component
VERIFIED: handleSendMessage in Messaging.js is wrapped with useCallback (lines 153-171) to prevent unnecessary re-renders
VERIFIED: TherapistDashboard.js (lines 84-103) correctly extracts selectedClientId from location.state and sets navContext
VERIFIED: TherapistDashboard.js (lines 349-351) passes navContext to SessionNotes, AIClinicalSupport, and Messaging for mobile secondary views
VERIFIED: Mobile back button (line 324-331) correctly navigates back to client profile if navContext.selectedClientId exists
VERIFIED: SessionNotes.js accepts navContext prop (line 37) and pre-selects client from navContext.selectedClientId (lines 85-91)
VERIFIED: Messaging.js accepts navContext prop (line 57) and pre-selects conversation from navContext.selectedClientId (lines 94-108)
VERIFIED: ai-clinical/index.js accepts navContext prop (line 22) and pre-selects client from navContext.selectedClientId (lines 92-103)
VERIFIED: ClientDashboard.js handlePrintDiagnosticReport (lines 306-398) uses iframe approach for PDF download instead of window.open to avoid popup blockers
VERIFIED: PDF download creates hidden iframe, writes HTML content, triggers print dialog, and cleans up iframe after delay

VERIFIED: MobileTherapistView.js More tab (lines 275-315) includes all secondary navigation items: homework-templates, resource-library, availability, recurring, assistants, profile, settings, support
VERIFIED: MobileTherapistView.js handleMoreNavigate calls onViewChange (line 466-468) which maps to TherapistDashboard views

success_rate.backend: 100% - All tested API endpoints working (login, clients, diagnostic-reports)
success_rate.frontend: CODE REVIEW PASSED 100% - UI testing blocked by browser crashes

context_for_next_testing_agent: All code implementations verified correct via code review. Browser automation consistently crashes on therapist/client dashboard pages due to environment resource constraints. Login flow works. Recommend manual testing or environment with more resources. The fixes include: 1) MessageInput memo component for typing lag, 2) navContext for client context preservation in mobile navigation, 3) iframe-based PDF download, 4) More tab navigation to secondary views.

Backend API - Login (curl).notes: Both therapist (7275005007) and client (9235555549) login successful, returns valid JWT tokens

Backend API - Clients (curl).notes: GET /api/clients returns 2 clients including Suman (test client)

Backend API - Diagnostic Reports (curl).notes: GET /api/diagnostic-reports returns 1 shared report for client Suman accessible by both therapist and client

Browser - Therapist Login (Playwright).notes: Login form fills correctly, redirects to /therapist

Browser - Client Login (Playwright).notes: Login form fills correctly, redirects to /client

Browser - Dashboard Navigation (Playwrig.notes: Browser crashes after successful login when loading dashboard components

MessageInput Component (Code Review).notes: React.memo wrapper with local state prevents parent re-renders from causing typing lag

Mobile Navigation Context (Code Review).notes: navContext properly passed from TherapistDashboard to SessionNotes, Messaging, AIClinicalSupport

PDF Download Iframe (Code Review).notes: ClientDashboard uses iframe approach instead of window.open to avoid popup blockers

More Tab Navigation (Code Review).notes: All secondary views (homework-templates, resource-library, availability, recurring, assistants, profile, settings, support) accessible via More tab
